headline: QUALCOMM (QCOM) Leaves Russell Growth Indexes, Is The Stock Cheap Or Pricey?
summary: QUALCOMM (QCOM) has just been removed from several Russell growth and defensive indices, a mechanical shift that can affect passive fund flows and how the stock is grouped alongside other semiconductor and AI companies. See our latest analysis for QUALCOMM. QUALCOMM’s latest index removals come after a strong run, with the stock posting a 50.30% 90 day share price return but giving back some ground with a 13.64% 30 day share price decline, while the 1 year total shareholder return stands at...

headline: This Semiconductor ETF Is Up 58% This Year and Doesn’t Own TSMC
summary: The VanEck Fabless Semiconductor ETF (NASDAQ:SMHX) has ripped higher in 2026, gaining 58.48% year to date through July 6, and it does not own a single share of the world’s largest chipmaker. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM), the foundry that physically fabricates chips for nearly every marquee AI silicon designer, is absent by design. That absence ... headline: Up 14.81% in a Year, COWZ Proves You Don’t Need Apple
